title: Calculate a deadline date in a column
excerpt: Hi. I’m looking to do something that should be (and therefore hopefully is) very simple. When an item is created on a board it sets a date column with a “received date” of today(). Currently I have a “Due date” which is …

posts.author: rt-ops (Andy)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi. I’m looking to do something that should be (and therefore hopefully is) very simple. When an item is created on a board it sets a date column with a “received date” of today(). Currently I have a “Due date” which is a formula based on using ADDDAYS to set a date x days in the future. However, this is entirely useless as it transpires a date in a formula column can’t be used for pretty much anything.

Is there a way to create a workflow or similar such that when the item is created and the Received Date is set, the Due Date is then updated to Received Date + x days?

I’ve looked at dependency columns but this doesn’t seem to do what I need. Any advice welcomed.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yep! You’ll need to do an automation of ‘when item created, set due date to today and then push date by x days’

image790×479 23.3 KB

Desiree - www.thecleverclovers.com

posts.author: Avo_FluidApps (Avo)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Andy rt-ops - you’re right that formula-based dates can’t trigger automations, but there’s a simple solution.

If you’re open to apps, there’s a workaround, you can use this automation from the Smart Timeline + Duration app:

When an item is created,
set smart timeline to start today and end/due in number days.

This sets your “Received Date” as today and automatically calculates the “Due Date” by adding X days without needing formulas.

If you want those dates stored in regular Date columns, there’s another automation:

When the smart timeline is set,
update the start and end date columns accordingly.

That way, you can use the dates in calendar views, reminders, and other automations.

There are over 20 automation recipes you can explore to support flexible workflows like this.

Let me know if you’d like help getting it set up!

posts.author: rt-ops (Andy)
posts.cookedHtml: That’s great - thank you. “Push date” was the action I was clearly missing!
